title: Evaluating AI driven crypto strategies with real time automation
author: EasternBaby2063
selftext: I’ve been experimenting with AI models for trading crypto, focusing on how automated systems handle volatility and regime shifts. One interesting observation is that combining predictive models with automated execution can reduce slippage, especially in fast moving markets but I just saw Yeagers, which lets users maintain custody while running automated strategies, providing a practical environment to test these approaches without relying on third party control. Curious how others incorporate risk metrics and feature selection into AI driven automation frameworks insights and experiences welcome.

comments.author: rosapigorsch
comments.body: One thing that’s helped me with regime shifts is treating volatility as a first class feature rather than a filter. Models that adapt position sizing based on recent volatility tend to survive market transitions better than those that just predict direction.

comments.author: Ricki008
comments.body: I agree on automation reducing slippage, especially during fast candles. I’ve seen similar behavior when testing on non custodial setups like yeagers, where execution logic can be separated from signal generation without giving up fund control. Risk rules end up mattering more than raw predictions.

replies.author: steak12356
replies.body: Feature selection feels underrated in AI trading discussions. Removing correlated indicators improved my live results more than switching models. Cleaner inputs seem to matter more than model complexity in crypto markets.

comments.author: DayNo4131
comments.body:
Foe feature selection, I’m using purged cross-validation with embargo, and I evaluate each feature in isolation.
For each feature I retrain a model (e.g. XGBoost) and use sample weights to account for label overlap, then score it across CV folds.
This is extremely computationally expensive since it’s basically a full training loop per feature, so you need serious compute (cloud or at least a high-end GPU like a 5090) to do it properly.
